title: THE COMMISSION
subtitle: The Cathedral · 10:24 AM · somebody asked for a piece
epigraph_upright: The Magician, commissioned, is a different animal. The tools are the same. The bench is the same. But the piece has an address now, and making toward an address is a magic with witnesses.
epigraph_reversed: You could make what they asked for. You could make what they meant. You could make what you wanted to make anyway and call it interpretation. Two of those are craft.
scene_description: Nicola asked — actually asked, money offered and refused, then offered again and taken because refusing twice is theater — for a piece: something for the shop counter, 'like the city stuff, but it can be small.' Morning light is in the cathedral doing its one good hour on the workbench. The steamboat sits where it sits, unfinished, watching you work on something that isn't it. Let it watch.
direction_hint: Make the piece for the address it has. Small is a size, not a compromise. Connect three, keep Doubt and Stagnation down, and leave the steamboat exactly as unfinished as you found it.
opening_log_lines: The 10 AM light hits the bench like a work order.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] for the rules. DRAFT toward the counter, not toward the floor city.
The steamboat is watching. It's allowed to watch. It is not allowed to vote.

stagnation_thresholds.label: the room is louder than usual
someone is crying in the bathroom

doubt_thresholds.label: is the work too small for them

notes: Fourth setup (Wave-3D, 2026-07-19) · mid-tier at a distinct hour. Wave-1 arcana: win/loss keys inherited VERBATIM from the medium sibling (bespoke engine schema); numbers between the easy and hard bookends.
ambient_pool: the show streams from the cassette deck. Elicia's voice, recorded, with you.
two conversations have crossed at the wheel-station.
someone is laughing too hard at something nobody else heard.
the critic is over by JUDGEMENT. don't look.
the superfan is whispering along to a passage.
outside, the river goes by at the rate it has gone by for fifty years.

scenario_visitors.name: Nicola's counter measurements, on a shop receipt
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Nicola measured the shop counter's free corner and wrote it on the back of a receipt: 9 by 6, 'and not taller than the register, it has to lose to the register.' The last constraint is the real commission — a piece that knows its place in someone else's room.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The measurements connect through FOCUS at the bench — build inside them. The constraint is the collaboration.

steps.greet: The receipt is in your pocket. 9 by 6. Has to lose to the register.
steps.listen: Read again, the numbers are a portrait of the shop: what the counter carries, where the light lands, what Nicola looks at during slow hours, which is where the piece will live.
steps.deliver: You draft inside the box. The piece gets better when the box pushes back. It always does and you always forget and the box always reminds you.
steps.sit_with: The finished draft sits inside 9 by 6 with air to spare, shorter than a register you've never measured but somehow know. It will lose to the register with grace. That was the assignment.
